name: Alexandru-Răzvan Olariu
education: Graduate Degree in Software Design & Engineering, Remote - Online Program from Massachusetts Institute of Technology; MSc Degree in Data Science from University of Portsmouth; MSc Degree in Internet of Things from Malmö University; Academic Program, Applied Sciences | .NET Core | Artificial Intelligence from Xamk - South-Eastern Finland University of Applied Sciences; BSc Degree in Business Informatics (Comp. Sci.) from Academia de Studii Economice din București; European Baccalauréat, Mathematics & Physics from National College ”Vlaicu Vodă”
jobTitle: M365 AI - Full Stack Software Engineer II
headline: Software Engineer
about:
Alexandru is a dedicated software engineer, recognized for his enthusiasm for technology and innovation across various IT sectors. His strong work ethic and results-driven approach highlight his exceptional potential, making him a valuable asset to any team he joins.

Alexandru's commitment to excellence and continuous learning propels him as a forward-thinking professional, ready to tackle challenges and drive innovation in the tech landscape.


——


A few key achievements that I'm very proud of : 
 
✅ Champion of Innovation: I've scored the 1st place at Microsoft's event held on the 7th of November 2020, named "Microsoft Student TECHathon" with a project titled "How can a startup reduce its costs and responsibilities by using cloud technologies?", leveraging the opportunities that are available in the Microsoft Azure cloud. 

✅ Mentor and Educator: I've offered free training sessions to students where I've talked about having Software Development as a hobby, what it means to work at one of the leading video-game companies (Ubisoft, 2020) and how to maintain faculty studies and duties. 

✅ Productivity Enhancer: By implementing Agile methodologies and personalized feedback sessions, I've boosted the productivity and efficiency of the Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors @ ASE team by an impressive 70% from previous years (internal survey + observed consistency - external, 2021).

✅ Recognized Talent: I've been awarded the top 100 at Sololearn's "Most Active Romanian Programmers of All-Time" (place #30) and at Hipo.ro's "Top Talents Romania 2021" (place #70) - an event dedicated to the best and most dedicated students of Romania. 


——


There's rarely a dull moment in my day-to-day life and I like it.
At the moment that you're reading this, I'm NOT open to work opportunities, remote work or office, full-time or part-time. 
 
▪ For an updated CV, please, contact me! ☺

currentPositions.description:
I'm part of the M365 AI Experiences organization, connecting people with AI.

Our team is pioneering model-forward design, embedding intelligent agents into everyday productivity tools like Copilot and Windows. I collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to prototype, evaluate, and scale AI-driven features that enhance user workflows and decision-making. 

I work in an organization that serves over 1 trillion (1T+) requests per day and provides up to six nines of reliability (99.9999x%).

I'm on a journey towards principal level engineering.

experiences.title: M365 AI - Full Stack Software Engineer II
experiences.jobDescription:
I'm part of the M365 AI Experiences organization, connecting people with AI.

Our team is pioneering model-forward design, embedding intelligent agents into everyday productivity tools like Copilot and Windows. I collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to prototype, evaluate, and scale AI-driven features that enhance user workflows and decision-making. 

I work in an organization that serves over 1 trillion (1T+) requests per day and provides up to six nines of reliability (99.9999x%).

I'm on a journey towards principal level engineering.

experiences.title: E + C - Sovereign Clouds Software Engineer II
experiences.jobDescription:
As governments today look at technology innovation, there is a need to address the rapidly evolving demands of their citizens while protecting the most sensitive data and delivering on promises of trust and security. 

Modernization of government services without compromising security, digitization of manual processes and improved user experiences for both citizens and government employees are all benefits of digital transformation, and the right technology platforms can create even better opportunities for social and economic growth.

Responsibilities:
▪Deliver one E+C Data Platform that is capable of running cross-clouds and cross-tenant in sovereign environments and air-gapped scenarios.
▪Manage and assure the wellbeing of the Runtime in Sovereign Environments (RISE) Data Platform operations.
▪Build a novel data mesh enterprise architecture using data engineering concepts, tools like Azure Data Factory and other data warehouse notions.

Achievements:
▪(Observability-as-a-Service) Fully orchestrated and implemented the three pillars of observability (3PO) in our microservices architecture using Open Telemetry standards; built automated monitoring and alerting triggers based on p95/p99 bucket statistics (metrics, spans, E2E flows).
▪(Research & Development) Assisted with major contributions to the development of an in-house .NET library that implements the ODataV4 data consumption protocol for NoSQL database engines such as Azure Data Explorer (ADX). 
▪(Development Experience) Took leadership of two key areas for DX: documentation & product adoption. Built outstanding documentation and leveraged static site generator tools (DocFX) to automatically generate API documentation from code; offered API consumption layers in form of micro-frontend UIs, PowerShell Cmdlets and OpenAPI spec files (Swagger / Postman).
▪(Growth) Shadowed and attended hiring interviews for SWE II and Senior SWE positions.

I was the lead driver for observability, documentation and CLI tooling.

experiences.title: CE&S UEX Azure Technical Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
With over 12,000 employees worldwide, the Microsoft Customer Experience & Success (CE&S) organization is responsible for the strategy, design, and implementation of the Microsoft end-to-end customer experience. 


Responsibilities:
▪Provide knowledge sharing, technical coaching and mentoring to fellow colleagues;
▪Problem solving through deep troubleshooting, debugging, log analysis, A/B testing;
▪Identify and respond to business critical customer issues specific to Azure services such as Azure Virtual Desktop;
▪Work exclusively with Fortune 500 customers to understand their technical needs, and define an action plan to meet them, collaborating with peers or other teams regularly.  

Achievements:
▪ Constantly pushed myself to achieve the best customer feedback. (Current score: 4.94 / 5.00)
▪ Created troubleshooting guides for the Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) solution.
▪ Currently working with Fortune 500 clients and industry leading professionals.
▪ Provided continuous feedback to the Product Group (PG) for Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) regarding the implementation of certain features and services.
▪ Developed the existing documentation about the features of Azure services such as Azure Virtual Desktop.

experiences.title: CE&S UEX Azure Technical Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Responsibilities:
▪Using technical expertise and excellent communication skills, Technical Engineers are responsible to fix real time issues, collaborating directly with the customers and other support professionals to help customers solve business problems. 
▪Technical Engineers are great problem solvers with keen analytical skills, who love working with industry leading technologies and are excellent communicators, asking good questions and demonstrating empathy.
▪Interns have qualified project management skills – ability to plan, execute and forecast actions in advance;

Achievements:
▪ Learnt how to use advanced Azure concepts such as Azure Active Directory Domain Services.
▪ Gained experience in using Microsoft PowerApps & Business Intelligence software.
▪ Developed a ticketing system from scratch with my intern colleagues on Azure.
▪ Deployed a fully-operational Azure Virtual Desktop lab for testing purposes.
▪ Successfully delivered 3 (three) soft-skills presentations to my colleagues.

experiences.title: Embedded Systems Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
The Timisoara site addresses several focus areas where software for various hardware components, image processing algorithms, and low-level and high-level implementations of component parts of neural networks are actively being developed.

Responsibilities:
▪Mastering skills in the world of image processing. 
▪Gaining a grasp of the domain of Machine learning.
▪Practice the achieved knowledge by developing a color space converter.

Achievements:
▪ Developed color space converters (CSC) in C and Assembly x8086 (YUV->RGB, Grayscale).
▪ Created 10+ types of image filters such as Fish Eye, Sobel, Edge Detection, Box Blur, etc.
▪ Refactored .ASM code to better perform on IoT & Embedded Systems devices.
▪ Learnt how to do Image Processing using Artificial Intelligence (open-cv).

experiences.title: QA/QC Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
I've worked on the Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora™ first person, action-adventure game built using the latest iteration of the Snowdrop engine (C++) by Massive Entertainment – a UBISOFT studio, and tested by UBISOFT Romania.

Responsibilities:
▪ Managed regression, integration and unit testing with software such as XRAY, TestRail, Bugzilla.
▪ Create high quality test suites and test plans using Atlassian (JIRA / Confluence) products.
▪ Assure communications are stable and clear between production and testing teams.

Achievements:
▪ I've consistently over-exceeded the quota of reported bugs, glitches and artefacts, ranking as the #1 reporter for a solid 5 months out of the 6 worked.
▪ Found and reported major code defects that ranged from race conditions to memory resource allocation defects.
▪ Increased the daily report efficiency by leveraging SQL and JQL filtering capabilities.

experiences.title: .NET Developer (Intern)
experiences.jobDescription:
PentaStagiu is a free IT training program developed by Pentalog more than 10 years ago to support tech enthusiasts in their journey to becoming proficient in various frameworks and technologies.

We started with on-site programs in Iasi, Chisinau, and Brasov. When we introduced remote training, the program became available to the entire tech community.

Responsibilities:
▪ Provide constructive feedback on the whole "PentaStagiu" experience.
▪ Debug & decompile code using ILSpy and Visual Studio 2019.
▪ Complete assignments on time and submit clean code.

Achievements:
▪ Successfully implemented a full-stack system management solution using ASP.NET and LINQ (local db).
▪ Learnt the fundamental concepts of the MVC architecture.
▪ Gained good knowledge in the .NET ecosystem.

updates.postText:
As the end of the year concludes, I am happy to say that I've achieved a streak of 15 back-to-back weeks where I've logged on Pluralsight to hit my study targets.

In this year, I've:
- followed 18 content authors.
- watched ~150 hours of video content.
- honed my knowledge in Networking, .NET and Software Development Lifecycle.

I've also did 2 Skill IQ tests this year: 
- Networking Fundamentals: Expert with 214 points.
- Building web applications with React: Expert with 201 points.

Thank you Theodor Cara for the trust you've given me in this learning journey. Sylvia Lau - carry on the torch in 2023!

#pluralsight #skilling #learneveryday #networking #react

I am proud to announce that I've successfully completed the IELTS Academic examination with an overall score (listening + reading + writing + speaking) of 8 out of 9.

IELTS is accepted by more government agencies, educational institutions, and professional bodies than any other English-language test in the world.
IELTS Academic examination assesses your English-language proficiency at an academic level to determine whether you are ready to study at a post graduate level, or work in a professional setting.

#ielts #examination #language

volunteerAndAwards.description:
Volunteer for Oracle Corporation.

- 9-11 May '25: Bucharest Half-Marathon - over 10.000 participants.
- 4 May '25: Star Wars event for children and adults with disabilities - over 80 participants.
- 20 December '24: Letters to Santa - gifts for young children in need - over 30 participants.
- 5 October '24: RUN for LIFE charity marathon - over 300 participants.
- 3 September '24: School supplies for kids - over 300 participants.
- 24 August '24: Life in nature - event for children with disabilities and orphans - over 30 participants.


---
Helped over 1.000 children and young adults have a better life.
Raised over 100.000 EUR for the following causes:
- disability awareness and help
- incurable diseases research
- education for endangered communities

volunteerAndAwards.organization: BCS, The Chartered Institute for IT
volunteerAndAwards.description: I act as a mentor and trainer for young individuals that are starting their journey in academia.

I act as a mentor for several students that come from endangered societies.
I offer my time and abilities for them to become community leaders in their societies.

I act as a general volunteer.

Activities done by me:
- I head over to specific rural villages to help the people there with:
--- 1) Food supplies
--- 2) Clothing supplies
--- 3) Personal advice
--- 4) Practical activities
- I act as a backup teacher.

Projects I took part in, while being in Codette:
    ▪ Fundraising Lead @ Web Superheroes (2021: February -> August): charity project which successfully aimed to teach certain aspects of web development to 24 children from a foster care. 
    ▪ Infrastructure Lead @ 2NHack Hackathon (2021: November): diversity and inclusion oriented hackathon which aims to leverage aspects of Neural Networks, Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to create a better world.

Main responsibilities in Codette @ Fundraising:
    ▪ Successfully signed contracts worth over 10.000 EUR with tech-oriented companies.
    ▪ Created and offered custom benefits packages for the Web Superheroes project.
    ▪ Kept partners and stakeholders with up-to-date project information.

Main responsibilities in Codette @ Infrastructure:
    ▪ Worked with other Codette's departments to provide a unified web experience.
    ▪ Developed web applications with a highly trained team, for internal usage.
    ▪ Created in-depth tutorials and guides on how to achieve more (growth mindset).
    ▪ Lead a department of ~10 individuals to achieve more and deliver consistently.

▪ Lead a team of 20 student volunteers for Microsoft Student Partner local division.
▪ Introduced & managed technical & feedback training sessions for volunteers.
▪ Integrated Agile methodologies in our projects.
▪ Increased the internal teams efficiency by 70% (see above points).
▪ Led internal teams to achieve consistently and foster a growth mindset.

▪ Managed, documented and reviewed existing translations for my native language.
▪ Offered high quality technical translations for educational open source software.
▪ Contributed to LibreELEC's Operating System Romanian language localization.
▪ Increased the general availability of over 10 open source software products.

recommendationsReceived.name: Theodor Cara
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Director, Windows Commercial Support at Microsoft
recommendationsReceived.description: While working together, Alex was an amazing support engineer with a complete customer centric approach in his daily activities. I was always impressed by the self drive Alex had in learning as much as he could during his spear time in order to become a better version of himself. Due to his great technical skills, he was always able to build confidence with the customers and to defuse any difficult situation that he was facing. Another great skill of Alex is his critical thinking, always challenging the status quo and making sure we take all inputs into account when making a decision. I believe Alex would make a great fit in any software engineering working environment.

recommendationsReceived.name: Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: QC Project Lead at Ubisoft
recommendationsReceived.description: During his stay inside our team, Razvan proved to be a competent and fast learning colleague. He was dedicated, outspoken, curious and capable of performing any task that was given to him. While our time working together was relatively short (cca. 6 months), he showed great potential in developing his skills, both soft and job related. I highly recommend Razvan for any employment he may seek inside his field of knowledge with the note that he is capable of learning areas outside his comfort zone.

summary: Alexandru-Răzvan Olariu is currently a Microsoft engineer and public-facing advocate who blends product and community-focused communications from within a large, market-leading technology company. His posts frequently cover developer tools and cloud topics—Visual Studio, .NET, C#, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot LM, Microsoft Fabric, Azure—and extend to telemetry and observability (OpenTelemetry + Grafana) as well as frontend ecosystem news (React Foundation). He mixes technical updates and thought leadership with personal career milestones (Pluralsight streaks, LinkedIn/IELTS achievements), past industry experience in QA/game development (Ubisoft), and active mentorship and event participation (DevTalks, Global Mentorship Initiative). His tone is professional, supportive and pragmatic: technical when explaining tools and integrations, encouraging when addressing students and mentees, and occasionally civic and reflective when sharing broader societal issues. Notable patterns include frequent sharing of product launches and ecosystem partnerships, practical how‑to or learning-oriented posts, public recognition of colleagues, and clear invitations for community interaction and outreach. This combination positions him as a developer-tools and cloud advocate who also offers hands-on credibility from QA/game-dev experience and visible mentorship/education contributions.
